name: Premier Detailing — 01 Lead to Quote & Booking

parameters.content:
## 01 — Lead → AI Quote → Booking + Deposit

**Trigger:** Wix form posts to the webhook below, OR Instagram/Facebook DM (via Meta webhook), OR an inbound SMS to the Twilio business number.

**Flow:**
1. Normalize the lead
2. Geocode the city → distance from Stratford
3. Pull next 7 days of available slots from Google Calendar (8hr crew cap)
4. Claude AI Agent generates a structured quote (uses prompts/quote-agent.md)
5. Decision: auto-book (≤30km) | owner-review (30–45km) | decline (>45km)
6. Auto-book path → Square Appointment + Square deposit link + Calendar event + SMS confirmation
7. Owner-review path → Slack/email alert with all context, no SMS to customer yet

**Voice:** see prompts/voice-style-guide.md — every customer-facing SMS goes through the agent so it sounds like Charlie/Kabir.

**Credentials needed:** Anthropic API, Twilio, Google Calendar, Square, Google Maps Geocoding, Slack (or Gmail).

## Lead form fields expected

```
first_name, last_name, phone, email,
year, make, model,
services[],   // multi-select IDs from data/pricing.json
addons[],     // multi-select IDs
city, postal,
photos[],     // optional URLs
notes,        // free text
preferred_date  // optional ISO date
```

Wix sends this as JSON. Custom form on book.premierdetailing.ca posts here too.

parameters.text:
=Lead intake payload:
```json
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}
```

Pricing reference (services + addons by vehicle size S/M/L/XL):
```json
{{ $('Load Pricing JSON').first().json }}
```

Generate the structured quote response per your system prompt. Return JSON only.

options.systemMessage:
=See prompts/quote-agent.md — paste full contents here.

The voice rules from prompts/voice-style-guide.md are also part of your behavior.

ALWAYS return strict JSON matching the output schema. No prose outside JSON.

parameters.content:
## Auto-book path (≤30km, all auto-quotable)

After the quote SMS goes out, this branch waits for the customer's reply. The reply arrives via the **Twilio inbound webhook** (separate workflow `04-inbound-sms-router` — see SETUP.md).

For the v1 build, the customer clicks the **Square booking link** in the quote SMS, which:
1. Charges the $50 deposit
2. Triggers a Square webhook → workflow `05-square-booking-confirmed`
3. Creates the Google Calendar event and sends confirmation SMS

This keeps workflow 01 simple: it ends after quoting. Confirmation is its own short workflow.

parameters.text:
=*New booking quoted — auto-book path*

*Customer:* {{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.customer.first_name }} ({{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.customer.phone_e164 }})
*Vehicle:* {{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.vehicle.year }} {{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.vehicle.make }} {{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.vehicle.model }} ({{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.vehicle.size }})
*Total:* ${{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.totals.total }} CAD ({{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.travel.fee }} travel)
*Slots offered:* {{ $('Parse Quote JSON').first().json.slots_offered.map(s => s.human).join(' / ') }}

parameters.message:
=hey {{ $('Compute Distance & Travel Fee').first().json.first_name }} its charlie. {{ $('Compute Distance & Travel Fee').first().json.city }} is a bit outside our usual range so i want to look at it personally before booking. ill text you back tonight with a real answer -- not a maybe.

-- charlie

=hey {{ $json.first_name }}, charlie here.

unfortunately {{ $json.city }} is past our service area for mobile this season. when we open the shop in stratford (target: summer 2027) we'd love to have you. ill add you to the list so you hear from us first when that happens.

cheers
